⭐ Task  🚧 In Progress  ⌛ Fri, 26 Jun 26  👷 Unassigned  🔑️ KAN-64  💭 0 comments  🧵 0 linked                   
                                                                                                                      
  # Throw typed ApiError across the apiFetch seam                                                                     
                                                                                                                      
  ⏱️  Thu, 25 Jun 26  🔎 Jae Yoon Bae  🚀 Medium  📦 None  🏷️  architecture, tech-debt  👀 You are watching           
                                                                                                                      
  ------------------------ Description ------------------------                                                       
                                                                                                                      
  From architecture review (deepening opportunity #5, Worth exploring).h2. Problem                                    
                                                                                                                      
  apiFetch (api.js) is the one real seam in the frontend, but it throws a formatted string - throw new Error( API     
  ${res.status}: ... ) at api.js:66 - so screens recover the status code by string-matching: Login.jsx:26             
  (err.message.includes('401')), Signup.jsx:32 (includes('409')). A stringly-typed leak across an otherwise clean seam.
                                                                                                                      
  ## Solution                                                                                                         
                                                                                                                      
  Throw a typed ApiError { status, code } from apiFetch; callers branch on err.code. The seam already exists - only   
  the                                                                                                                 
  value crossing it changes.                                                                                          
                                                                                                                      
  ## Wins                                                                                                             
                                                                                                                      
  • stops the stringly-typed leak                                                                                     
  • small: one throw site, two callers                                                                                
  • pairs naturally with a useAsyncAction module for the loading/error/saving triple repeated across ~6 screens       
                                                                                                                      
  ## Acceptance criteria                                                                                              
                                                                                                                      
  • apiFetch throws ApiError with structured status/code                                                              
  • Login and Signup branch on code, not on err.message.includes(...)                                                 
  • No remaining .includes('401') / .includes('409') status checks in screens                                         
  • App click-through: 401 still redirects, 409 still shows the conflict message

  # Give the wearable-core rule a single home (assembleOutfits module)                                                
                                                                                                                      
  ⏱️  Thu, 25 Jun 26  🔎 Jae Yoon Bae  🚀 Medium  📦 None  🏷️  architecture, tech-debt  👀 You are watching           
                                                                                                                      
  ------------------------ Description ------------------------                                                       
                                                                                                                      
  From architecture review (deepening opportunity #4, Worth exploring). Pairs with the runTool seam ticket.h2. Problem
                                                                                                                      
  The wearable-core rule is written three times in style-pieces/style-pieces.service.ts - prompt prose at L71,        
  regenerate                                                                                                          
  prose at L140, and code at L343-345 (dress || (top && bottom)). Prose and code can drift, and the dropped-outfit    
  logic                                                                                                               
  is buried inside the 96-line composeFromPrompt, so the one piece of real domain logic (what makes an outfit valid)  
  has no module of its own and no direct test.                                                                        
                                                                                                                      
  ## Solution                                                                                                         
                                                                                                                      
  A pure assembleOutfits(raw, lookup) module resolves IDs, applies hasWearableCore(), and drops incomplete looks. The 
  prompts cite the same named rule. Once runTool owns the SDK call, composeFromPrompt splits cleanly into "call the   
  model" + "assemble".                                                                                                
                                                                                                                      
  ## Wins                                                                                                             
                                                                                                                      
  • single source for the wearable-core rule                                                                          
  • pure module: trivially testable, no SDK needed                                                                    
  • interface is the test surface                                                                                     
                                                                                                                      
  ## Acceptance criteria                                                                                              
                                                                                                                      
  • hasWearableCore + outfit assembly live in one pure function/module with direct unit tests                         
  • The completeness rule is defined once and referenced by the prompts                                               
  • style-pieces.service delegates assembly to it                                                                     
  • npm run test green

⭐ Task  🚧 In Progress  ⌛ Fri, 26 Jun 26  👷 Unassigned  🔑️ KAN-62  💭 0 comments  🧵 0 linked                   
                                                                                                                      
  # Extract useAvatarRender - one render-or-cache seam for all 3 screens                                              
                                                                                                                      
  ⏱️  Thu, 25 Jun 26  🔎 Jae Yoon Bae  🚀 Medium  📦 None  🏷️  architecture, tech-debt  👀 You are watching           
                                                                                                                      
  ------------------------ Description ------------------------                                                       
                                                                                                                      
  From architecture review (deepening opportunity #3, Strong).h2. Problem                                             
                                                                                                                      
  Three call sites each re-implement the same flow - build key -❭ check cache -❭ (peek) -❭ generate -❭ store - with   
  their own                                                                                                           
  status field and key shape. The free peekAvatar (pay-nothing) path exists in only one of them, so cache keys can    
  drift.                                                                                                              
                                                                                                                      
  • Wardrobe.jsx:85 (useRef cache + local renderStatus)                                                               
  • Discover.jsx:561,839,857,948 (Map in DiscoverContext; peek at :839)                                               
  • LookSpread regenerate variant                                                                                     
                                                                                                                      
  ## Solution                                                                                                         
                                                                                                                      
  One useAvatarRender(items) module owns the key, the cache, the peek-before-generate, and the status. The three      
  screens                                                                                                             
  call it.                                                                                                            
                                                                                                                      
  ## Wins                                                                                                             
                                                                                                                      
  • three adapters: the seam is overdue                                                                               
  • leverage: one cache key shape across all screens                                                                  
  • peek-before-pay applies everywhere, not just results                                                              
  • one render status machine instead of three                                                                        
                                                                                                                      
  ## Acceptance criteria                                                                                              
                                                                                                                      
  • A single useAvatarRender hook/module encapsulates buildKey + cache + peek + generate + status                     
  • Wardrobe, DiscoverResults, and LookSpread all consume it; no screen builds its own avatar cache key               
  • Peek-before-generate is used in all three                                                                         
  • App click-through still renders + caches avatars

⭐ Task  🚧 In Progress  ⌛ Fri, 26 Jun 26  👷 Unassigned  🔑️ KAN-61  💭 0 comments  🧵 0 linked                   
                                                                                                                      
  # Collapse Discover.jsx pipeline orchestration into a runPipeline module                                            
                                                                                                                      
  ⏱️  Thu, 25 Jun 26  🔎 Jae Yoon Bae  🚀 Medium  📦 None  🏷️  architecture, tech-debt  👀 You are watching           
                                                                                                                      
  ------------------------ Description ------------------------                                                       
                                                                                                                      
  From architecture review (deepening opportunity #2, Strong).h2. Problem                                             
                                                                                                                      
  The backend deliberately keeps each pipeline stage standalone, so the whole 4-stage funnel lands in one 1071-line   
  component (screens/Discover/Discover.jsx) wired straight to six endpoints, mutating a 17-atom context               
  (context/DiscoverContext.jsx). There is no locality: which stage feeds which, and what to stash for regenerate      
  (groupedItems, styledVibe), is reconstructed by reading the component top to bottom across handleSubmit,            
  confirmVibe, and handleRegenerate.                                                                                  
                                                                                                                      
  Call sites: extractVibe (Discover.jsx:521), findPieces (:542), stylePieces (:546), generateAvatar (:561,857,948),   
  peekAvatar (:839), regenerateOutfit (:942).                                                                         
                                                                                                                      
  ## Solution                                                                                                         
                                                                                                                      
  A deep runPipeline module owns the stage sequence and the intermediate handoffs. Discover shrinks to a view that    
  starts the pipeline and renders pipeline.state; DiscoverContext shrinks to a result cache.                          
                                                                                                                      
  ## Wins                                                                                                             
                                                                                                                      
  • locality: funnel bugs concentrate in one module                                                                   
  • AI-navigable: ~400 lines instead of 1071                                                                          
  • handoffs (groupedItems, styledVibe) become the interface, not smeared state                                       
  • one place for retry / error recovery                                                                              
  • pipeline testable without the DOM                                                                                 
                                                                                                                      
  ## Acceptance criteria                                                                                              
                                                                                                                      
  • A pipeline module owns extractVibe -❭ findPieces -❭ stylePieces -❭ generateAvatar sequencing + regenerate handoff 
  • Discover.jsx no longer calls the six api functions directly                                                       
  • DiscoverContext only holds form inputs + session results                                                          
  • App still runs end-to-end (npm run lint && npm run build, click-through)

⭐ Task  🚧 In Progress  ⌛ Fri, 26 Jun 26  👷 Unassigned  🔑️ KAN-60  💭 0 comments  🧵 1 linked                   
                                                                                                                      
  # Deepen the Anthropic tool-use call into one runTool module (testability seam)                                     
                                                                                                                      
  ⏱️  Thu, 25 Jun 26  🔎 Jae Yoon Bae  🚀 Medium  📦 None  🏷️  architecture, tech-debt  👀 You are watching           
                                                                                                                      
  ------------------------ Description ------------------------                                                       
                                                                                                                      
  From architecture review (deepening opportunity #1, Strong).h2. Problem                                             
                                                                                                                      
  The Anthropic tool-use dance is byte-identical in two services, and the SDK client is built inline in each          
  constructor                                                                                                         
  (new Anthropic()). Because the call is fused into the service, neither service has a unit spec - only the           
  controllers                                                                                                         
  are tested, with the whole service mocked. The interface to test against does not exist.                            
                                                                                                                      
  Duplicated boilerplate:                                                                                             
                                                                                                                      
  • extract-vibe/extract-vibe.service.ts:70 (client), :136-141 (find tool_use then throw 502)                         
  • style-pieces/style-pieces.service.ts:38 (client), :279-306 (find tool_use then throw 502)                         
                                                                                                                      
  ## Solution                                                                                                         
                                                                                                                      
  One deep module behind a narrow runTool(toolDef, content) interface that absorbs: client construction,              
  messages.create, tool_use extraction, the BAD_GATEWAY throw, and (newly) retry. Two adapters justify the seam - real
  Anthropic in prod, an in-memory fake in tests.                                                                      
                                                                                                                      
  ## Wins                                                                                                             
                                                                                                                      
  • interface is the test surface: extract-vibe + style-pieces become unit-testable                                   
  • two adapters make the seam real (prod Anthropic / fake)                                                           
  • retry stops being avatar-only; one place for SDK quirks (locality)                                                
  • delete two copies of the 502 path                                                                                 
                                                                                                                      
  ## Acceptance criteria                                                                                              
                                                                                                                      
  • A single provider exposes runTool and is injected into both services                                              
  • extract-vibe.service and style-pieces.service no longer call new Anthropic() directly                             
  • New unit specs for both services pass against a fake tool-runner (no live API key)                                
  • npm run test green                                                                                                
  Unblocks the wearable-core rule ticket (deepening #4).                                                              
                                                                                                                      
  ------------------------ Linked Issues ------------------------                                                     
                                                                                                                      
  BLOCKS                                                                                                              
                                                                                                                      
  KAN-63 Give the wearable-core rule a single home (assembleOutfits module) • Task • Medium • Idea

  ------------------------ Description ------------------------                                                       
                                                                                                                      
  Update the CSS for all frontend screens to be responsive at mobile viewport widths (375 px minimum). No Tailwind —  
  use CSS media queries in each screen's colocated .css file.                                                         
                                                                                                                      
  Screens to audit and update:                                                                                        
                                                                                                                      
  • frontend/src/screens/Auth/ (login, signup)                                                                        
  • frontend/src/screens/Discover/Discover.css (main pipeline view)                                                   
  • frontend/src/screens/Wardrobe/Wardrobe.css                                                                        
  • frontend/src/screens/Onboarding/                                                                                  
  • frontend/src/screens/Paywall/Paywall.css                                                                          
  • frontend/src/screens/Settings/                                                                                    
  • frontend/src/screens/Looks/                                                                                       
                                                                                                                      
  ## Context                                                                                                          
                                                                                                                      
  The app currently targets desktop. Mobile users see broken layouts. Mobile-friendly presentation is a prerequisite  
  for any user acquisition on mobile platforms.                                                                       
                                                                                                                      
  ## Acceptance Criteria                                                                                              
                                                                                                                      
  • All screens render without horizontal scroll or overlapping elements at 375 px viewport width                     
  • Desktop layout at 1280 px shows no regressions                                                                    
  • All layout adjustments are in colocated CSS files using @media queries; no layout logic or inline style added to  
  JSX                                                                                                                 
  • npm run build in frontend/ passes

  [ ] What is the primary mobile breakpoint to target (375 px iPhone SE, 390 px iPhone 14, 430 px iPhone Pro Max)?    
  [ ] Is a simple linear-stack/reflow approach sufficient, or is there a separate mobile design to follow?

  # Styling guide                                                                                                     
                                                                                                                      
  ⏱️  Thu, 25 Jun 26  🔎 Jae Yoon Bae  🚀 Medium  📦 None  🏷️  None  👀 You are watching                              
                                                                                                                      
  ------------------------ Description ------------------------                                                       
                                                                                                                      
  Collect user styling preferences (body attributes and style persona) during onboarding and personalise outfit       
  recommendations accordingly.h2. Context                                                                             
                                                                                                                      
  Currently the pipeline applies no user-specific filtering beyond gender. Personalising by body type, skin tone, and 
  age range is the product's stated USP. No new DB fields, onboarding steps, or prompt changes exist yet.             
                                                                                                                      
  ## Likely affected areas                                                                                            
                                                                                                                      
  • **New DB fields** on the users table ( backend/prisma/schema.prisma ): e.g. age_range , body_type , skin_tone     
  • **Onboarding screens** in frontend/src/screens/Onboarding/ to capture the new fields and persist via updateMe()   
  • find-pieces ( backend/src/find-pieces/find-pieces.service.ts ) and/or style-pieces ( backend/src/style-           
  pieces/style-pieces.service.ts ) to consume the stored attributes                                                   
  • **Settings screen** ( frontend/src/screens/Settings/ ) — optionally allow updating attributes after onboarding    
                                                                                                                      
  ## Needs human input                                                                                                
                                                                                                                      
  [ ] Which attributes should be collected and what are their allowed values? (e.g. age range: under-25 / 25-35 / 35- 
  50 / 50+; body type: petite / straight / hourglass / athletic / plus; skin tone: …) Please provide exact field names
  and value sets.                                                                                                     
  [ ] How should the attributes influence recommendations — injected into the style-pieces prompt, added as catalog   
  filters in find-pieces , or both?                                                                                   
  [ ] Should users be able to edit these attributes in Settings after onboarding, or only set them once?

  # FE: PostHog initialization and event tracking                                                                     
                                                                                                                      
  ⏱️  Thu, 25 Jun 26  🔎 Jae Yoon Bae  🚀 Medium  📦 None  🏷️  None  👀 You are watching                              
                                                                                                                      
  ------------------------ Description ------------------------                                                       
                                                                                                                      
  Install and initialize PostHog in the frontend so that user-flow events reach the PostHog analytics dashboard.h2.   
  Context                                                                                                             
                                                                                                                      
  PostHog is not yet initialized in the frontend — no posthog-js package exists and no posthog.capture() calls are    
  present. This instrumentation is required before the A/B paywall experiment (KAN-53) can be configured.             
                                                                                                                      
  ## Changes                                                                                                          
                                                                                                                      
  • frontend/package.json — add posthog-js dependency                                                                 
  • frontend/src/main.jsx — initialize PostHog with VITE_POSTHOG_KEY and VITE_POSTHOG_HOST env vars; skip             
  initialization when the key is absent (local dev / CI)                                                              
  • frontend/src/analytics.js (new) — thin track(event, props) wrapper so screens don't import PostHog directly       
  • **Individual screens** — call track() at the moments listed below                                                 
                                                                                                                      
  ## Events to instrument                                                                                             
                                                                                                                      
   **Event**                                               | **Location**                                             
  ---------------------------------------------------------|--------------------------------------------------------  
   pipeline_started                                        | Discover.jsx — user submits the vibe form                
   vibe_extracted                                          | Discover.jsx — vibe profile returned                     
   outfits_styled                                          | Discover.jsx — outfit cards rendered                     
   outfit_saved                                            | Wardrobe screen / save action                            
   paywall_viewed                                          | Paywall.jsx — on mount                                   
   checkout_started                                        | Paywall.jsx — user clicks "unlock my closet"             
   signup_completed                                        | Auth/Signup.jsx — registration succeeds                  
                                                                                                                      
  ## Acceptance Criteria                                                                                              
                                                                                                                      
  • posthog-js added to frontend/package.json                                                                         
  • PostHog initialized in main.jsx ; no error thrown when VITE_POSTHOG_KEY is absent                                 
  • All events in the table above fire at the correct moments in the running app                                      
  • npm run build in frontend/ passes                                                                                 
                                                                                                                      
  ------------------------ Linked Issues ------------------------

  instrumentation (FE + BE)        • Task • Medium • In Progress

  # A/B test paywall variants via PostHog feature flags                                                               
                                                                                                                      
  ⏱️  Fri, 19 Jun 26  🔎 Jae Yoon Bae  🚀 Medium  📦 None  🏷️  analytics, backlog  👀 You are watching                
                                                                                                                      
  ------------------------ Description ------------------------                                                       
                                                                                                                      
  Using PostHog feature flags, set up a 2–3 variant A/B test over the paywall screen. Each variant differs in headline
  copy and/or displayed price. Variants are assigned client-side via a PostHog flag evaluated on paywall load. Track  
  paywall_viewed , checkout_started , and subscription_completed events with a variant property on each.              
                                                                                                                      
  Depends on: Stripe paywall live (KAN-43), PostHog instrumentation live (KAN-51).                                    
                                                                                                                      
  ## Context                                                                                                          
                                                                                                                      
  Provides data-driven optimisation of paywall conversion after the initial paywall and analytics are live. Even a    
  small lift in conversion rate compounds significantly with user acquisition scale.                                  
                                                                                                                      
  ## Acceptance Criteria                                                                                              
                                                                                                                      
  • PostHog feature flag created with 2–3 named variants (e.g. control , price-anchor , social-proof )                
  • Paywall screen reads the active variant from PostHog and renders the corresponding copy and/or price display      
  • paywall_viewed event sent with {{{ variant }}} property on every paywall load                                     
  • PostHog experiment or funnel dashboard shows conversion rate per variant                                          
  • If the PostHog flag call fails or times out, the screen falls back to the default variant without error

⭐ Story  🚧 Testing  ⌛ Tue, 23 Jun 26  👷 Unassigned  🔑️ KAN-47  💭 0 comments  🧵 0 linked                      
                                                                                                                      
  # Onboarding: brand picker grouped by category                                                                      
                                                                                                                      
  ⏱️  Fri, 19 Jun 26  🔎 Jae Yoon Bae  🚀 Medium  📦 None  🏷️  frontend, sprint-1  👀 You are watching                
                                                                                                                      
  ------------------------ Description ------------------------                                                       
                                                                                                                      
  Update frontend/src/screens/Onboarding/BrandSelection.jsx so brands are rendered under fashion-category headers (e.g.
  Streetwear, Y2K, Old money, Minimal) instead of a flat alphabetical list.                                           
                                                                                                                      
  The brand list comes from GET /brands (backed by backend/src/brands/brands.service.ts which reads distinct brands   
  from catalog_items ). Category membership must be defined in a new static config file — e.g.

  # Stripe account, products, pricing setup (test mode)                                                               
                                                                                                                      
  ⏱️  Fri, 19 Jun 26  🔎 Jae Yoon Bae  🚀 Medium  📦 None  🏷️  monetization, sprint-1  👀 You are watching            
                                                                                                                      
  ------------------------ Description ------------------------                                                       
                                                                                                                      
  Set up the Stripe account in test mode with the narcie subscription product and pricing. These credentials are      
  required by the backend checkout endpoint and webhook handler (both shipped in KAN-42 and KAN-43).h2. Steps         
                                                                                                                      
  1. Log in to the Stripe Dashboard (dashboard.stripe.com) and switch to **test mode**                                
  2. Create a **Product** : "narcie premium" (recurring subscription)                                                 
  3. Create a **Price** : $10 USD/month recurring (confirmed by product owner)                                        
  4. Create a **Webhook** endpoint for https://❬your-domain❭/stripe/webhook , subscribing to checkout.session.        
  completed and customer.subscription.* events                                                                        
  5. Copy the following into backend/.env and the Cloud Run service env:                                              
  6. STRIPE_SECRET_KEY — from Stripe Dashboard → Developers → API keys                                                
  7. STRIPE_WEBHOOK_SECRET — the webhook endpoint's signing secret                                                    
  8. STRIPE_PRICE_ID — the Price ID from step 3 (starts with price_ )                                                 
  9. Test end-to-end using Stripe test card 4242 4242 4242 4242                                                       
                                                                                                                      
  ## Context                                                                                                          
                                                                                                                      
  KAN-42 (webhook entitlement check) and KAN-43 (paywall screen) are both Done. The full checkout flow cannot run in  
  production until the live Stripe keys and price ID are in the Cloud Run env. Price confirmed as $10 USD/month.      
                                                                                                                      
  ## Acceptance Criteria                                                                                              
                                                                                                                      
  • Stripe product and monthly recurring price ($10 USD) exist in the test-mode dashboard                             
  • STRIPE_SECRET_KEY , STRIPE_WEBHOOK_SECRET , and STRIPE_PRICE_ID are set in backend/.env                           
  • POST /stripe/checkout-session returns a valid Stripe-hosted checkout URL in test mode                             
  • After completing with test card 4242 4242 4242 4242 , the user's subscription status is updated to active via the 
  KAN-42 webhook handler                                                                                              
  • frontend/src/screens/Paywall/Paywall.jsx:151 displays the same price configured in Stripe (currently shows        
  €9/month EUR — see open question)                                                                                   
                                                                                                                      
  ## Needs human input                                                                                                
                                                                                                                      
  [ ] The Stripe price is confirmed as $10 USD/month, but Paywall.jsx:151 currently shows "€9/month (was €14, founders
  rate)". Should this file be updated to show "$10/month" as part of this ticket, or is a separate ticket needed? (If 
  left as-is, the last AC above will not pass.)

Set up the Stripe account in test mode with the narcie subscription product and pricing. These credentials are required by the backend checkout endpoint and webhook handler (both shipped in KAN-42 and KAN-43).

## Steps

1. Log in to the Stripe Dashboard (dashboard.stripe.com) and switch to **test mode**
2. Create a **Product**: "narcie premium" (recurring subscription)
3. Create a **Price**: $10 USD/month recurring
4. Create a **Webhook** endpoint for `https://<your-domain>/stripe/webhook`, subscribing to `checkout.session.completed` and `customer.subscription.*` events
5. Copy the following into `backend/.env` and the Cloud Run service env:
   - `STRIPE_SECRET_KEY` — from Stripe Dashboard → Developers → API keys
   - `STRIPE_WEBHOOK_SECRET` — the webhook endpoint's signing secret
   - `STRIPE_PRICE_ID` — the Price ID from step 3 (starts with `price_`)
6. Update `frontend/src/screens/Paywall/Paywall.jsx:151` to display "$10/month" (currently shows "€9/month (was €14, founders rate)")
7. Test end-to-end using Stripe test card 4242 4242 4242 4242

## Context

KAN-42 (webhook entitlement check) and KAN-43 (paywall screen) are both Done. The full checkout flow cannot run in production until the live Stripe keys and price ID are in the Cloud Run env. Price confirmed as $10 USD/month.

## Acceptance Criteria

- Stripe product and monthly recurring price ($10 USD) exist in the test-mode dashboard
- `STRIPE_SECRET_KEY`, `STRIPE_WEBHOOK_SECRET`, and `STRIPE_PRICE_ID` are set in `backend/.env`
- `POST /stripe/checkout-session` returns a valid Stripe-hosted checkout URL in test mode
- After completing with test card 4242 4242 4242 4242, the user's subscription status is updated to active via the KAN-42 webhook handler
- `frontend/src/screens/Paywall/Paywall.jsx:151` displays "$10/month"
